title: vigna appendiculata
intro: Vigna appendiculata, also known as the hairy cowpea or hairy bean, is a type of climbing plant native to tropical Africa. It is a member of the legume family and is known for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and digestive issues, and is also believed to have cardiovascular benefits due to its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Vigna appendiculata can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder, and is also used in traditional African medicine to treat a range of ailments.
